2026-04-23T09:49:30.4342065Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4342491Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4342826Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4343140Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4343393Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4343588Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4343722Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4343881Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4344027Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4344216Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T09:49:30.4344623Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T09:49:30.4344833Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-23T09:54:33.6313429Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T09:50:30.323091544Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58591,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] Docs: Link new client-options.md from index and README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57924 by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57924 added `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/client-options.md` — a new reference document describing all supported `@`@clientOption`` keys for the Azure management-plane C# emitter. The document covers two decorator keys:\\n- `resource-rbac-roles`: defines RBAC role definitions for provisioning code generation\\n- `resource-name-constraint`: overrides resource name length/pattern constraints when spec decorators can\\u0026#39;t be used without causing breaking changes\\n\\nThe emitter source files (`resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`) were updated to read the new `resource-name-constraint` client option.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx` has no link to the new `client-options.md` file — users browsing the docs hub will not know it exists\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` has no mention of `@`@clientOption`` support or a pointer to `client-options.md`\\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md` also has no cross-reference to the new `@`@clientOption`` documentation, even though `@`@clientOption`` keys directly influence resource code generation driven by the emitter\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Edit `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/index.mdx`\\n\\n**Edit** this file to add a link to the new `client-options.md` alongside the existing emitter link.\\n\\nReplace the existing `## Emitter usage` section footer:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n```\\n\\nwith:\\n\\n```mdx\\n## Emitter usage\\n\\n[See documentation](./emitter.md)\\n\\n## Supported `@`@clientOption`` Keys\\n\\nThe emitter recognizes several `@`@clientOption`` keys that provide additional metadata for resource code generation.\\n\\n[See documentation](./client-options.md)\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Edit `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** the README to add a reference to the `@`@clientOption`` doc. Append a new section after the emitter options block (before the end of file):\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys\\n\\nThe emitter recognizes several `@`@clientOption`` keys (scoped to `\\u0026#34;csharp\\u0026#34;`) that allow spec authors to provide additional metadata driving resource code generation — for example RBAC role definitions and resource name constraints.\\n\\n[See the full reference](docs/client-options.md)\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Edit `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/emitter.md`\\n\\n**Edit** this file to add a cross-reference at the end, after all the emitter option entries:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Supported `@`@clientOption`` keys\\n\\nIn addition to emitter options, the management emitter reads per-resource metadata from `@`@clientOption`` decorators in the TypeSpec spec.\\n\\n[See full `@`@clientOption`` reference](./client-options.md)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo snippet changes are required — this is a documentation-only update with no new test-backed code samples.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe `README.md` for this emitter package is a tool/generator README (not a client SDK README), so the standard Getting Started / Key Concepts / Examples / Troubleshooting / Next Steps / Contributing template does not apply directly. The current structure (Install → Emitter usage → Emitter options) is appropriate. The only gap is the missing `@`@clientOption`` reference section added in Step 1b above.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; npm install`\\n2. `npm run lint` — verify no linting errors in emitter source\\n3. Manually verify that the markdown links resolve correctly (e.g., `./client-options.md` from `index.mdx` and `emitter.md`, and `docs/client-options.md` from `README.md`)\\n\\nNo dotnet build, test, or snippet/API export steps are needed for this documentation-only change.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt;
